title: The activity displayed in the calendar activity is the creation date, not the activity date
excerpt: The activity displayed in the calendar activity is the creation date, not the activity date. How to display the activity date in the calendar activity, not the creation date?

posts.author: fradella (Fradella Sulivan)
posts.cookedHtml:
The activity displayed in the calendar activity is the creation date, not the activity date. How to display the activity date in the calendar activity, not the creation date?

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @fradella , Welcome to the monday.com Community!

You Have to add a Field “Activity Date” in the board to track any updates on calendar. And then from the setting of calendar view you have to select that Field.

By default ,monday cant select any option whether creation date or activity date.

Zaki
Business Workflow Consultant
Ready logic

posts.author: Ag47Panda (Steve Wasson)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes. Agreed with @ZakiImtiaz Monday can’t use those autogenerated fields like last updated and what not in the calendar tool. Set up a simple automation where when the thing is done they click DONE or whatever in a STATUS field and then have the automation date and time stamp in date/time fields you create. Then they can be used in the calendar.

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @fradella,

If you ever want to display your activity start time and end time as a block in the calendar, you can use Calendar Plus. Having a single start+end time is currently part of the free plan.
